name: default
description: Machine-readable summary sibling of the research report: the research question, a findings array (each claim with its sourcing evidence), an options array (2-4 options with pros/cons), and a single recommendation block (chosen option, rationale, key trade-off). Placeholder strings keep the file parseable; the agent expands the findings/options exemplar elements, one per claim/option.

slots.description: A factual claim found in the codebase. The findings array (see shape) carries one exemplar element to clone per finding.
The file path or line number that sources the claim.
Option label, e.g. 'A', 'B'. The options array (see shape) carries one exemplar element to clone per option.
What this option costs or risks.
The chosen option's label or name.
What you're optimizing for vs. what you're giving up.

shape.findings[]: { claim: string, evidence: string (file path or line) }
shape.options[]: { label: string, name: string, pros: string, cons: string }
shape.recommendation: { option: string, rationale: string, key_tradeoff: string }
